childCosts.note: Regional, school-type, and activity-level variance is enormous. Treat as a rough ceiling, not a forecast. Centrelink subsidies can substantially reduce net cost.

generalLifeCostShifts: Discretionary spending typically declines 20-30% when a child is born, then recovers over the first 2-3 years.
Housing costs typically peak between ages 35-50 as mortgage principal is paid down.
Healthcare out-of-pocket costs accelerate after age 55, particularly dental and specialist care.
Transport costs often fall in retirement as commuting disappears, though regional residents may see no change.
Insurance premiums rise materially through the 60s across life, income-protection, and health.
Aged-care entry, when it happens, is typically funded by a mix of super drawdown, home equity release, and the age pension — cash savings alone rarely suffice.
